As an SAP BTP architect, there are different tools and assets available to support you in your role. Do you know them all? Do you even know where to start? Probably not. That’s exactly where the SAP BTP Guidance Framework comes in. From decision guides and methodologies to SAP BTP reference architectures, we’ve compiled all the must-have information in one place. This makes it easier for you to find what you need, understand why you need it and successfully accomplish your tasks. You can best use the guiding assets of SAP BTP Guidance Framework together with SAP LeanIX enterprise architecture management solutions which allow you to plan, govern, and track the modernization of your IT landscape according to best practices from SAP. 

Hint: this session will also include a brief overview of the SAP BTP Guidance Framework. If you are completely new to the concept, even if you are not an architect, I highly recommend attending this first session.

😎  Meet our experts: @KatrinVonAhsen and @Rene_de_Daniel 

📆  Thursday, January 30 at 3PM CET

➡️  Register here 

📺  Watch live on YouTube.

Episode 2: Paving the way for developers to innovate with SAP BTP

This session will focus on developers and how they can benefit from the SAP BTP Guidance Framework. 

Developers have many tasks and interact with various puzzle pieces as they navigate through the ins and outs of their development projects. Without a clear understanding of what is available, then it could get messy and complicated very quickly. But worry not, the SAP BTP Guidance Framework is the answer to it all. It’s the single source of truth for all relevant SAP BTP assets and sources of information that you’ll need to successfully complete your development tasks. 

Our expert will share how and why we compiled these assets and dive further into the SAP BTP Developer's Guide and SAP Build. Both essential for kicking off any and all development projects on SAP BTP.

😎  Meet our expert: @srivatsansundar1 

📆  Tuesday, February 4 at 4PM CET

➡️  Register here

📺  Watch live on YouTube

Episode 3: Paving the way for administrators to operate efficiently with SAP BTP

With episode 3, we’ll turn our focus to the unsung heroes of day-to-day operations – the administrators.

Administrators need to deal with several different tasks that span from onboarding to SAP BTP, to running and operating applications as smoothly as possible on the platform. This includes concepts like account structuring, deployment and observability and understanding how to use OpenTelemetry@SAP through SAP Cloud ALM for operations.

But administrators are also responsible for enabling their teams to efficiently use SAP BTP. For example, by enabling agile development on SAP BTP and ensuring the right level of control.  

And last but not least, it’s important to understand how SAP supports administrators with tools, best practices and guidance. For instance, by reducing manual efforts around account provisioning and operating applications, and by integrating SAP BTP operation processes into a central operations platform like SAP Cloud ALM. 

Our experts will share how all this and more is covered in the SAP BTP Guidance Framework with assets like the SAP BTP Administrator Guide and the new SAP learning journey “Administrating SAP Business Technology Platform”. 

😎  Meet our experts: @BorisZarske, @stephaniejerch and @NiklasSiemer  

📆  Thursday, February 13 at 4PM CET

➡️  Register here 

📺  Watch live on YouTube.

💥  Will you be attending? Do you already have questions for our experts? Let us know in the comments! 💥

entry.title: Four Clean Core Use Cases for SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Business Technology Platform
entry.content:
Introduction

Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems have long been adapted to match companies' distinct business processes. However, as customizations accumulate, organizations are finding that maintaining their ERP functions becomes more complex, often slowing down innovation.  

In this short blog post, I’ll cover four use case examples with SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) you can adopt to simplify ERP upgrades with BTP extensions, while reducing testing time and effort. Customer examples are provided for each scenario, as well as links to technical implementation steps.

  1. Extend SAP S/4HANA processes without impacting core functionality
  2. Connect employees and partners to delight your customers
  3. Simplify process connectivity to any application
  4. Enhance SAP S/4HANA with planning and analysis

About the author

Terry Penner is part of the SAP BTP product marketing team, focused on BTP for SAP S/4HANA. He has more than 20 years of technical and business experience in the SAP platform and analytics, including working directly on hundreds of customer implementations.

Use case #1: Extend SAP S/4HANA processes without impacting core functionality

 

Innovative companies need to give their teams the tools to quickly turn great ideas into real-life changes. But for most businesses, it's still challenging to allow business users to create applications efficiently while maintaining IT governance standards.

Use SAP Build Apps so your SAP S/4HANA users can create extensions quickly without impacting core functionality.  Business users want to create specific workflows quickly without writing code, all while following IT company standards. For example, your team can design mobile and desktop applications and speed up development with prebuilt connectors and business content for SAP and third-party systems.

Freudenberg Group  adopted a low-code development strategy to 200 business users to help the company more quickly digitalize processes. This approach replaced manual paper document exchange, boosting new technology adoption fivefold. Now, the company is expanding this initiative to build a community inside Freudenberg for digital builder to solve key challenges and drive sustainable, value-driven growth.

>> Learn how to implement this use case

 

Use case #2: Connect employees and partners to delight your customers

The Lead to Cash process crosses multiple applications and connects employees and partners from initial interactions to order fulfillment. Real-time inventory levels and locations are vital, ensuring both sales reps and distribution partners have current data, especially when they may be competing for the same inventory.

Use pre-built integrations from SAP Integration Suite (part of SAP BTP) to connect SAP S/4HANA, finance and supply chain systems. As a result, your employees can boost business efficiency and provide a superior customer experience – all without requiring additional IT resources.

Vorwerk transformed the person-to-person sales experience by digitalizing the direct selling process. The world’s largest direct seller of premium household appliances used SAP Integration Suite and SAP BTP to connect back-end systems from SAP and third parties. This greatly reduced sales paperwork and significantly accelerated the sales cycle for their advisors.

>> Learn how to implement this use case

 

Use case #3: Simplify process connectivity to any application

Running a business on siloed legacy systems is no longer a competitive option.  A connected landscape is required to meet employee and customer expectations for simplified mobile access, omnichannel interactions, and collaborative engagement.

Open Connectors, a core component of SAP Integration Suite, offers over 160 prebuilt, feature-rich connectors to help ensure smooth connections with third-party SAP applications. The component helps create an integrated API layer and supports standards-based implementation for SAP and third-party applications, regardless of the back end. It facilitates critical digital capabilities, including mass-updating asset master data, generating purchase requisitions originating from a spreadsheet, and uploading manual entries automatically through an API.

Elkjøp is a prime example of a company leveraging this use case to build integrations with third-party applications. Using SAP Integration Suite, the consumer electronics retailer created an event driven architecture to connect services, repairs, installations, capacity handling and support tickets. The company processes 5 million messages daily to provide an experience where a customer can place an online order and pick it up in-store within 30 minutes.

>> Learn how to implement this use case

 

Use case #4: Enhance SAP S/4HANA with planning and analysis

Effective use of financial data safeguards your business from damaging risks, such as maverick spending, regulatory noncompliance, and reporting errors. But it also optimizes a finance organization's already limited time and energy.

By enabling native connectivity to SAP S/4HANA Cloud data from SAP Analytics Cloud, you can create a fully integrated foundation for aligning your operations and finance strategies. Business leaders can use financial planning and analysis to gain a real-time, business-wide understanding of critical business drivers to mitigate potential risks early, address planning variances proactively, and improve profitability. Company specific financial planning can be accomplished while still allowing for easier updating of SAP S/4HANA core modules.

Take Watco, a leading transportation and logistics provider in the United States. Watco brought together data from more than 200 different profit centers to provide a consolidated company view of financials. This allows the company to analyze different data sets to provide improved forecasting and a better link between financial planning and ERP. 

>> Learn how to implement this use case

 

Innovation without compromising ERP integrity

With SAP BTP together with SAP S/4HANA, it is possible to have the ERP experience that meets your business needs today, tomorrow, and in the future.  By applying these four use cases, you can help ensure the ERP system operates efficiently and supports the innovation of digital capabilities unique to your business. You can design custom applications, create connected experiences, simplify connectivity, and align operations and finances – all without compromising the integrity of your ERP investment.

For more use cases that can unleash your company’s potential, learn more about SAP Business Technology Platform with SAP S/4HANA.

 

Explore more related content in this series about SAP BTP with S/4HANA:

Overview

  • 2024 SAP BTP Year in Review: Release Highlights for SAP S/4HANA users

Artificial Intelligence

  • Artificial Intelligence (AI) for SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP: A deep dive
  • How SAP Business AI and Large Language Models are shaping the future of SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP 

Data Migration

  • SAP S/4HANA Data Migration and Master Data Management Best Practices with SAP BTP

Extensions

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions with SAP Build Best Practices: An Expert Roundtable
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions and Clean Core with SAP BTP - A Best Practices Conversation

entry.title: SAP Developer News, January 23rd, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

Podcast: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/01/23/sap-developer-news-january-23rd-2025/

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 2025 Developer Insight Survey

1:14 Code Connect 2025

2:32 New Release of the SAP Cloud SDK for AI

3:17 “Wait for an API Call” Step in SAP Build Process Automation

4:23 New SAP Community interest group - Integration

4:47 New Certification Prerequisite for SAP Build Low-Code Certification

Transcription

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for January 23rd, 2025.

[Riley] The annual SAP Developer Insight Survey is live now. This is the sixth annual running of the survey for us. If you've taken it before, you'll see many familiar questions, things that we use to track trends over time. There's also a deeper exploration this year of topics like generative AI for developers and also developer enablement. In the description section below, you're going to do that. to find two links. The first is actually a link to last year's report. If you haven't looked at that yet, I think you'll find the results interesting. The second link, of course, is to this year's survey. It will take about 12 to 15 minutes of your time to complete. The survey is going to be open for about six weeks. At the end of that time, we'll go off and analyze the data and produce a report that we'll share with you. So I look forward to seeing your comments at the point that we do that. In the meantime, dive into the links, explore it, and we appreciate your feedback. It's important because it helps us shape the programs that we use within the community for 2025. Thank you.

[DJ] Good morning. Storm Éowyn is on its way. It's already bitterly cold. It's starting to rain and the winds are gathering. But I've just got time before I got on my run to tell you about the grassroots annual SAP Tech Conference of the year. That is, of course, Code Connect. Happy way again this year, in the usual place, certainly unruped, in Germany, near Walldorf. In the usual time of year, it's the first full week in July, and we have two usual suspect one-day conferences, the awesome recap and the amazing UI5con, and Code Connect is joined this by a new conference HANA TechCon. So we're also planning to run a couple of CodeJams on the Monday, the first day of the Code Connect week. So let us know if you're interested in those too. For those traveling from afar, there's also an early bird ticket application process. Anyway, all the links are in the description. I'm going to be there and I hope to see you there too.

[Thomas] Hey, I wanted to tell you about the new release of the SAP Cloud SDK for AI. This is a great tool for JavaScript developers to be able to build your own custom AI orchestration services and interactive applications. In this new 1.6 release, we see the inclusion of image recognition in the orchestration service, as well as screaming. capabilities in the orchestration client. So if your JavaScript developer looking to get started with SAP AI capabilities, be sure to check out this new release of the SAP Cloud SDK for AI. AI.

[Daniel] SAP Build Process Automation is introducing a new type of process step. Wait for an API call. The step lets you pause a process automation. and then let an external system call an API to resume it. When you add this type of step to your process, you are creating a new trigger. But instead of this trigger creating a new process instance, it merely resumes an existing instance that had already been paused by the step. The idea is to allow process automation to integrate with external systems and let those systems take over part of the process and then signal back to the process instance that it has finished. It seems like it'll open up a world of possible integration scenarios with process automation. I've written a blog on how to use it, step by step, and I will leave a link to the blog post in the description.

[Antonio] Holla, SAP developers. Did you know that there is a new interest group in SAP community which focuses completely on the integration topic? This is our place to share and connect with a collaborative community of integration developers. Now, what are you waiting for? Go and join a new group. I'm including the link in the description.

[Thomas] I'd like to tell you about a new edition into the certification experience, particularly for SAP build low code no code certification. SAP is adding a new hands-on practical portion of the certification experience. This is a free learning course that comes with system access to allow you to execute exercises to get hands-on practical experience. This is part of a new effort by SAP to bring more practical capabilities and testing as part of the certification experience. So check out the links in the show notes for the addition to the certification, the particular certification that's involved, as well as the free experience learning journey that you can take to get ready for that certification.

entry.title: Get the menu to the top of Business Application Studio
entry.content:
So a new Development Space has a default that may not be in your personal favour. Here a way to change things.

Go to "Settings" and search for Classic

Change entry from compact to classic

Select visible

and you end with this now:

Hope you find your prefered settings. Somehow this gets overwritten from time to time. This is fast way to change things.

BR Volker

entry.title: How can SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite support your journey to SAP S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
Yesterday, while presenting SAP’s financial results for FY2024, our CEO Christian Klein explained that we exceeded our cloud goals, accelerating cloud revenue and current cloud backlog growth against a much larger base. He also mentioned that half of our cloud order entry are now including AI. Clearly, our aim is to provide our customers with a suite of cloud-based applications that provides the most valuable data in business and powers unique AI capabilities. In this blog post, I would like to present how SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite – both running on SAP Business Technology Platform – are at the heart of SAP’s strategy and support our customers in their journey from legacy ERPs to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.


We know that for many SAP customers, the transition to SAP S/4HANA can be challenging, because someone in the organization has added a lot of custom code to your ERP 😉. That’s why SAP has created RISE with SAP, a packaged offering that helps you transform on-premise SAP ERP systems, modernize business processes, and become more agile in the cloud. And in RISE with SAP, you will find anything you need to fulfill your needs.

Let me be a bit more specific: every company in the world uses software to run their operations. We all know the most efficient and effective way is to use standard software like our SAP S/4HANA ERP. At the same time, we are fully aware that every organization in the world is unique and gains competitive advantage through their very specific processes. This might be the way they run processes for their customers because they offer unique experiences, or the way they engage with their suppliers because they may be faster in the supply chain processes, or it might be their HR processes which allows them to retain the best talent in the market… whatever it is, that makes your company unique, it should be reflected in your software because no one likes manual processes in 2025. In the context of migrating from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, following the Clean Core strategy is a powerful opportunity: by eliminating custom code, technical debt, and standardizing the processes and data that underpin ERP operations, your business will benefit from more best practices while being able to customize what it needs to differentiate itself. And when it comes to standardization to achieve clean core with proper extensions and integrations, SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite on SAP BTP enter the game!

SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite add great value to your Cloud ERP

SAP Build allows you to extend and personalize SAP S/4HANA to meet your needs. It accelerates development and automation of extensions with low-code, pro-code, and generative AI tools. It enables stable, secure, and scalable ERP extensions that adhere to a prescribed extensibility model, with compliance to code quality standards and best practices. Tailored for every SAP customer, any size, any landscape, any industry, SAP Build is the best of suite tools for developers and key users collaborating in AI-powered ERP extension development.

Let me say it simply: SAP Build adds great value to your Cloud ERP. You will get more out of your cloud ERP investment with SAP Build. Just because it lets you easily customize, extend, integrate and automate business processes in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud solution to meet specific requirements while keeping the core clean. Watch our webinar “Extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Build: your Free Toolkit”: in an hour, you will discover how you can leverage SAP Build’s low-code, pro-code, and generative AI development tools to drive innovation, automate processes, and supercharge your S/4HANA Cloud experience.

SAP Integration Suite is an iPaaS that helps you quickly integrate on-premises and cloud-based processes, services, applications, events, and data. For sure, it also adds value to your ERP: it connects business processes with proven pre-built integrations, APIs, connectors, and best practices. It provides upgrade-stable interfaces, proper monitoring and error resolution capabilities, and actively used and well-documented connectors.

For What Use Cases?

Let me give you very concrete examples where SAP Build is very helpful to address specific challenges in your Lead to Cash processes:

  • Digitize in-store cash sales processing
  • Automate the creation of sales orders from Excel files
  • Automate mass maintenance of billing blocks
  • Automate the approvals of return requests
  • Automate the creation of sales orders from unstructured data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Automate managing credit blocks on sales orders

and more…

I can also share many use cases that could improve your Source to Pay processes:

  • Centralize and streamline supplier communications with dedicated business sites
  • Mass maintenance of purchase contracts
  • Automate the creation of purchase requisitions from structured data in SAP S/4HANA
  • Automate the processing of paper-based invoices
  • Automatically populate missing material codes in purchase requisitions
  • Track sustainability data across your supply chain

and more…

You will find complementary use cases on our SAP Build web page.

And there is more: on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, you can browse 500+ pre-built content packages for SAP Build and 600+ pre-built content packages for SAP Integration Suite. If you have developed custom code in your on-premises ERP in the past to address such use cases or specific needs, you can explore this vast catalog to see if some of these packages might be a good fit for your cloud ERP migration process. Indeed it is very important to know that SAP Build comes with pre-built content for ECC: you can use these SAP Build packages on ECC already to standardize some extensions, and then all the extensions developed on ECC with SAP Build will taken to S/4HANA during the migration.

Insights from Customers

What I presented above is not only theoretical: customers who have been through this SAP S/4HANA journey share valuable insights that can really help you. I have selected two few interesting stories, in different industries.

Sony Pictures

Let me start with Sony Pictures Networks: relying on the legacy system, SAP ECC, their highly customized and siloed infrastructure struggled to align with the company's integrated business processes. And decentralized data presented SAP challenges in consolidating business reports until the end.

Sony decided to remaster their operations for the future through next-generation ERP, with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and Enterprise Automation, combining SAP Signavio, SAP Build And SAP Integration Suite. Indeed, SAP Signavio radically accelerates business process transformation, and I engage you to read this blog post explaining how customers on SAP ECC systems can prepare for an SAP S/4HANA transformation with SAP Signavio.

“By migrating to SAP S/4HANA and adopting SAP Business Technology Platform, we can keep the core clean. This strategy allows for faster development of any new technologies or any new process rollouts and enables us to take advantage of generative AI as part of our way forward” explains Mahesh Patil, SVP and Head of IT – Infrastructure, Integration, and Data Lake, Sony Pictures Networks India. And the results are excellent:

  • 100% automated process mining, reducing effort from weeks to days
  • 50% of interfaces managed through SAP Integration Suite
  • ~85% faster mobile app creation, reducing effort from four weeks to three days

Hitachi

Would you like another good example? Let’s take Hitachi High-Tech Corporation: they replaced their highly customized multinational business landscape by investing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud in a two-tier public and private cloud model. With a fit-to-standard ethos, the company can now focus on supporting business innovation across its subsidiaries using a side-by-side approach on SAP Business Technology Platform.

“Our new policy is to fit our business processes to standard as much as possible, reducing our need for customizations” says Yuri Yasuda, Manager Corporate DX Department, Digital Transformation Business Group, Hitachi High-Tech Corporation, who shares impressive KPIs:

  • 94% reduction in customization footprint
  • 18x faster handling of solution updates
  • 100% of on-site maintenance requirements eliminated

You can also find inspiration in the S/4HANA projects that Mahindra Group, PX Energy, LEOCH, Farys, Delta Light or Prestige have successfully implemented.

Start your S/4HANA journey with the support of SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite!

I hope that you’re now convinced that SAP BTP in general and SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite in particular are very powerful to ease your migration to SAP S/4HANA. To start this journey, watch our short video and request a customized demo: together, let’s make your business unstoppable!

entry.title: SAP Developer News, January 30th, 2025
entry.content:
Description

What’s New in SAP Build: Q4 2024 Webinar

  • Webinar Recordings (for Public) https://workzone.one.int.sap/site#workzone-home&/groups/zJfVxkPC5b2kQfs6YahqyX/content?folder_id=TfxCd7kH9hdfZ1hdvxFyBU&view_mode=list
  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-blog-posts/what-s-new-in-sap-build-q4-2024-edition/ba-p/13980162

SAP Build Code – What’s New January 2025

  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – January 2025 Edition: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/product-updates-for-sap-build-code-january-2025-edition/ba-p/13993021
  • Deprecation of Java Tools in SAP Web IDE: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/deprecation-of-java-tools-in-sap-web-ide/ba-p/13997766
  • SAP Build Code Joule Evolves: Unveiling Multi-Agent Support for Advanced CAP Application Generation: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-code-joule-evolves-unveiling-multi-agent-support-for-advanced-cap/ba-p/13995395

Upcoming CAP January Release 2025

  • npm @SAP/cds-dk https://www.npmjs.com/package/@sap/cds-dk
  • CAP Release page January 2025 | capire

SAP Community active on Bluesky social media

  • SAP Community Account on Bluesky: https://bsky.app/profile/sapcommunity.bsky.social
  • Starter Pack SAP Community:  https://bsky.app/starter-pack/sapcommunity.bsky.social/3lfnowo7ykb2l
  • Starter Pack SAP Developer Advocates:  https://bsky.app/starter-pack/sapcommunity.bsky.social/3lgbebsq43a2x
  • Starter Pack SAP Champions:  https://bsky.app/starter-pack/sapcommunity.bsky.social/3lgbesf6hqa2r
  • Starter Pack SAP Mentors:  https://bsky.app/starter-pack/sapcommunity.bsky.social/3lgbem6f4u32p

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 What’s New in SAP Build: Q4 2024 Webinar

2:13 SAP Build Code What’s New Jan 2025

5:11 Upcoming CAP January Release 2025

6:14 SAP Community active on Bluesky social media

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro]This is the SAP Developer News for January 30th, 2025.

[Shrini] Hello SAP developers. I hope many of you got a chance to watch the webinar yesterday on the new SAP Build features released in Q4 2024. There were updates on product-specific highlights on tools such as SAP Build Process Automation, build code, build apps, and WorkZone with some cool demos on the new features. Here are some of my favorites. The design time UI in build apps has been redesigned allowing for a more intuitive user experience. Different pages in an app are easily accessible via drop down. Less frequently used tasks are positioned under app settings. Preview and publish options are now more prominent. In the process automation, now you can create and manage process variants. Process variants allow a variant template developer to create processes with pre-configured settings and a variant management user, usually a business configuration expert can modify the template variant to suit his needs which changes like change or order of variance, modify the start condition, release and deploy variants, etc. In WorkZone standard edition, the integration of Joule is a game changer. Joule in WorkZone provides answers and insights from any connected system via prompts, eliminating the need to switch between different tools and user interfaces. Note that this feature is not available in the free trial. Joule integrated in SAP Build Code, now it's seamless migration of SAP UI5 applications from JavaScript to TypeScript. SAP Build Code has an adaptation editor tool which is a new tool for extending SAP UI5 applications. It replaces the SAP UI5 Visual Editor. There are many more features. release in the last quarter, do check out the webinar recording and the blog post explaining all the features, the link to which are in the description below.

[Thomas] My colleague, Shri has already brought you some of the news about SAP build enhancements that were released in January of this year. I want to do a little breakout there on specific enhancements for SAP build code. Of course, build code is part of SAP build build and a little bit. bit of the news was covered in his segment, but for professional developers, I want to give you a little additional information. First of all, in SAP Build Code, we see the new project overview. This is to really replace the project Explorer and give you a better visualization of your project and in particular match up the structure to what you're seeing when you're using the storyboard editor. Next, we see improvements when working with remote function calls. There we've added the ability for the run configuration to be updated automatically as well as support both basic and principle propagation. Next, we see the inline editing for CDS entities. This allows you to edit entities and their attributes without having to switch between the graphical editor and the text or code-based editor. And we see the deprecation of Java tools for SAP WebIDE Now, this is an important note because this has to do with the deprecation of the SAP Java BuildPack version 1. Now, that deprecation is going to take place in June of 2025, and at that time, the older tools in the SAP Web IDE for Java development will also be deprecated. Now, this just means that you need to be using the SAP Business Application Studio, which we recommend that you use anyway. So by June 2025, please plan on moving your CAP Java and other Java-based, uh, tooling-based projects to the business application studio. And finally, the last item has to do with the Joule code generative, uh, capabilities in SAP build code. We see the agent, uh, are the, Joule assistant going multi-agent with the latest enhancements, particularly we see this in the CAP, new project generation. And now what you can do is you can give it a robust description of what you want in your entire CAP application to do, and in a single prompt, it will respond by both generating the application, generating sample data, and generating application logic for what you described. This used to be several different calls and interactions that you'd have to have with the Joule assistant and now, with it being multi-agent, you can do that in all one prompt. So look for all these enhancements in SAP Build Code. And of course, the blog post are all in the links to the show note of this episode.

[Kevin] The January 2025 cap releases right around the corner and we got some great release notes for you. The new CDS parser replacing ANTLR is almost done. With the new parser, you will get reduced installations and faster parsing, as well as improved code completion. And with the new release, you will also be able to use enum symbols when defining a default value. More enum symbols support will come in the future. And on the Java side, you can now use predicates as select items to evaluate Boolean expressions on the database. Odata v401 allows you to invoke functions using implicit parameter aliases, and this invocation style is now also supported by the CAP Java runtime. And CDS debug has been extended for Java, so you can easily debug local and remote Java applications. And lastly, I want to mention that IntelliJ has now SAP CDS language support. There are even more great news about the upcoming release, and you can read all about them in detail on the CAP release page.

[Josh] Hey there, it's Mr. Bluebeard, and I hope you are enjoying the blue sky. It is very windy and cold. outside today, so I came inside to talk to you about Blue Sky, not that Blue Sky, but about the social media site, Blue Sky. SAP is getting more and more active on Blue Sky. We've got the SAP community there. They've had a great feature called starter packs. So if you don't want to follow just the SAP community site, but also a bunch of individuals within other groups, you can quickly grab a starter pack for something like the SAP mentors, champions, developer advocates, or you can just continue to follow individuals. I've got an account there along with a bunch of other people that are inside of those other groups. It's just a great place where information can go and live freely. It was started by some people that started at different social media site years ago. And this is just basically a reboot of a place to go for short, micro -blogging, links, pictures, videos to things you care about. So follow accounts like the SAP community or maybe the UI5 group if you're into that. So there are more and more groups popping up every day. And Blue Sky is not just a song by a great band called ELO Right, DJ? It's a great place to go and share information. We'll see you there. Bye.

entry.title: SAP BTP Hackathon at SAPHILA 2025 (AFSUG Members)
entry.content:
This year we launched the first-ever AFSUG and SAP collaboration SAP BTP Hackathon.

In a nutshell:

  • All AFSUG members can nominate a business use case that showcases SAP BTP capabilities
  • Up to 25 customers will be selected to build out their use case and compete for a chance to demo it live on stage at SAPHILA 2025
  • 8 finalists will participate in the first-ever SAPHILA Demo Jam in June for a chance to win some cool prizes

When talking to Amanda Gibbs, the CEO of the African SAP User Group (AFSUG), she shared her thoughts:

"The AFSUG SAP BTP Hackathon is an extraordinary opportunity for our members to showcase their creativity, problem-solving skills, and innovative thinking. By collaborating with SAP on the SAPHILA Hackathon, we’re creating a platform where customers and partners can explore the transformative potential of SAP BTP, turning bold ideas into tangible solutions that deliver real business value. At AFSUG, we believe in the power of community and shared learning. This hackathon is not just about competition; it’s about fostering connections, gaining insights, and driving meaningful change together. I encourage all our members to seize this chance to innovate, learn from SAP’s global experts, and be part of something truly impactful."

I really can’t beat that intro, so I am not even going to try 🙂

 

The Details

SAP BTP is THE platform that brings together application development, automation, data management, analytics and planning, integration, and AI capabilities into one unified environment optimized for integrating and extending SAP applications.

It’s used by customers, partners and SAP to innovate and optimize business processes while leveraging the latest and greatest cloud technology.

The SAP BTP hackathon challenge will showcase how customers can use SAP BTP to adapt and optimize their business processes. But that’s not all!

AFSUG members will also benefit from in-depth enablement sessions led by SAP product experts to help them through the ideation and build phases.

The participating customers, those who have had their use case selected for the SAP BTP hackathon, will also have access to expert coaches during their build phase. Coaches are there to make sure “no use case is left behind” 😉

Once the build phase is completed, customers will pitch their use case to a jury of SAP experts. The jury will choose 8 finalists based on different criteria, like business relevance, SAP BTP usage, innovation and vision, that will go on to demo their use case in front of a live audience at SAPHILA 2025.

To show off your efforts, participating customers will get special badges added to their SAP Community profile. So, if you are not yet an SAP Community member, what are you waiting for?

 

To All AFSUG Members

Don’t miss this unique opportunity to participate in the SAP BTP hackathon!

In short:

  1. Submit your use case here by February 18
  2. (Selected customers) Build your prototype by April 22
  3. Get ready to pitch your demo from May 2 to May 16
  4. (Finalists) Compete in the Demo Jam at SAPHILA 2025 on June 3

In case you still need convincing, let me reiterate some of the perks:

  • Attend SAP BTP enablement sessions led by SAP experts for inspiration and knowledge.
  • Engage with the SAP ecosystem and get special badges to display on your profile. Don’t forget to create an account in the SAP Community
  • Get direct access to SAP BTP coaches to support you during your build phase.
  • Expand your SAP BTP knowledge and bridge the gap between business and IT.

 

Conclusion

We are beyond excited to see what our customers come up and see their ideas come to life with SAP Business Technology Platform.

And yes, we would love to bring this format to other user groups and locations. One step at a time 😉

entry.title: Convert DMS Folder ID to Name in Automation
entry.content:
Many people are having trouble working with DMS (SAP BTP Document Management System) in automations because when a user uploads a document via a form, SAP Build Process Automation only passes the object ID of the folder.

Problem is, the Get DMS Folder Content activity in automations needs a path.

You can solve this problem with just a few extra activities – I've done it in 3 steps, but you can probably do it in fewer. Note that all this assumes that the folder is in the root, which is where SAP Build Process Automation puts uploaded files.

To start, you will need a destination to your DMS repo. I just use the same one that I use to enable DMS in SAP Build Process Automation forms, the one called sap_process_automation_document_store.

The URL for mine is in this form:

https://api-sdm-di.cfapps.us10.hana.ondemand.com/browser/<repoID>

You will need to create an environmental variable of type destination, used to select the DMS destination. I called mine MyDMS.

And you need is an input variable of type string to your automation.

This variable will take from your process the string representation of the DMS folder that contains your document. The folder will be in the root of the repo and will be in this format:

spa-res:cmis:folderid:PH_jHhsadhewQ5ijlsakvwRcCOx5SbQYq8zorhf0K-I

 

Custom Script

The custom script is used to provide the definition of the API call we will make in the next activity. 

The script will have these inputs and outputs.

You will have to bind the input to the automation input above of the same name.

Here is the script:

 

 

let options = {
    method: 'GET',
    resolveBodyOnly: true,
    responseType: 'json'
};

if (folderId){
    options.url = "/root?objectId=" + folderId.split('folderid:')[1] + "&cmisselector=properties";
}

return options;

 

 

It is based on the Get Properties API for DMS.

 

Call Web Service with Destination

Add the Call Web Service with Destination activity, and set it up like you would for most such calls.

  • Set the input destination to your environmental variable.
  • Set the options input to output of your custom script. 
  • Keep the default output object, of obj.

 

Create Variable (String)

Now extract the name of the folder. Add a Create Variable activity (actually done by dragging a String data type into the flow).

All you need to do is create an input and output.

The input is the following formula, based on the output of the Call Web Service activity.

Step3.obj["cmis:name"].value

And the output can be any name you want.

Now you have the folder name.

😺

 

 

 

 

 

 

.

entry.title: Supercharge efficiency with Enterprise Automation: SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
The ability to intelligently automate business processes is top of mind for many stakeholders involved in process management across organizations. According to a recent IDC study, annual spending on Intelligent Process Automation (IPA) technologies is projected to reach $102 billion by 2028. Of this, $19.9 billion will be allocated to AI-powered automation tools, with an impressive annual growth rate of 43.4%.

While there will be certainly no shortage in resources to spend for automation software, the real challenge remains in the details: How can organizations implement a holistic approach for process excellence, which allows them to manage processes, identify bottlenecks, deliver scalable automations and integrate their process extension within a holistic process landscape. In my previous blogpost I have already outlined in detail how SAP’s framework on Enterprise Automation delivers an solution to the problem statement mentioned above.

And this blogpost series will offer you a more detailed guidance on the interconnectivity of each service included in Enterprise Automation which are:

  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Integration Suite
  • and of course SAP Signavio.

Let’s explore the value you can unlock for your processes by combining SAP Build Process Automation with SAP Integration Suite in this one.

 

What is Enterprise Automation?

As outlined in my previous blogpost Enterprise Automation with SAP: Enterprise automation with SAP software provides an integrated set of tools for process mining, automation, integration, monitoring, and optimization of your processes and workflows. It helps you quickly and intuitively gain insights into your business processes and identify the right automation tool for the right outcome. While each solution can be leveraged independently, Enterprise Automation offer native integration between SAP Build, SAP integration Suite and SAP Signavio – supplemented with best practices, guidance and pre-build content which can be implemented directly.

 

 

  • SAP Signavio provides visibility into business processes and potential bottlenecks, root-cause analysis, and suggestions for improvement based on 200,000 industry benchmarks and best practices. This detailed understanding of inefficiencies across your organization enables you to optimize and automate processes.
  • SAP Integration Suite is an integration platform as a service with thousands of prebuilt integrations, APIs, events and connectors to SAP and third-party software applications. The suite reduces complexity across on-premise and multi-cloud environments, helping you optimize comprehensive business processes.
  • SAP Build to create automations and applications usingthe low-code and no-code design tools. Including pre-built automations and connectors for SAP software and third-party systems, the solutions let your business experts rapidly address process inefficiencies

 

Here you can also see a visualization of the main components and its respective capabilities:

 

Leverage the combined power of SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Integration Suite

1st scenario: Integrate to innovate

The first scenario we are going to cover in this blogpost is about building required process extensions and integrating the to your systems of records. SAP Build Process Automation allows you to create entirely new business processes and automation from scratch. However, during development, you may need advanced integration capabilities and various types of connectors to link your processes with different systems, whether cloud-based or on-premise, and whether using SAP or third-party platforms.

Integration Suite, you can natively trigger an iFlow created in SAP Integration Suite at any point within your process definition. This is particularly useful when handling tasks like data transformation, entity mapping, field validation, or accessing over 250 connectors such as iDoc, SOAP, SMTP, Kafka, and more. Moreover, customers that start their process excellence journey with Integration Suite can complement their integration styles with additional automation capabilities like approval workflows, business rules or RPA bots that have been provisioned with SAP Build Process Automation.

Let’s jump into an example. In the screenshot below you can see a process definition within Build Process Automation to automate the creation of Purchase Requisitions within your organization:

 

 

Once all the parameters and required approvals are fulfilled, we want to create a PR document within our system of record. In the first Action of the screenshot below you can see that we are natively calling an OData service to directly push this new PR within our SAP S/4HANA on-premise system. But in the second Action, we trigger an additional Integration Flow (iFlow) that has been established within the SAP Integration Suite:

 

 

This is done rather quickly thanks to the ability to natively trigger an iFlow within the Action design-time of SAP Build Process Automation, which is also documented in our Help-Portal:

 

 

This allows you to include in iFlow within your process definition whenever it is necessary. Because in the iFlow design-time of the Integration Suite you can establish any scenario that is relevant, make use of data validation and entity mapping, and use more than 250 different connectors to integrate to any backend application. The connectivity options and adapters are also documented within the Help-Portal. In this screenshot you can see how the iFlow replicates the PR document in our MM-module of SAP ECC by using a SOAP adapter:

 And here you can see an example how entity mapping looks like: We are mapping the corresponding data fields of the OData service to the SOAP service to make sure that no data gets lost or misinterpreted.

 

 

2nd scenario: Event-driven architectures

Another common use case for combining SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite is in the area of event-driven architectures. Thanks to the Event Mesh service available in SAP Integration Suite, you can listen to and subscribe to critical business events, whether they are happening within your SAP or non-SAP environment. This also includes the new Advanced Event Mesh service, which offers more capabilities, broader deployment options, and the ability to handle larger payloads compared to the regular Event Mesh service.

SAP Build provides native connectivity to both Event Mesh and Advanced Event Mesh, enabling you to proactively trigger process instances without human intervention, based on business events as they occur.

For instance, you can use Advanced Event Mesh to listen for and subscribe to the creation of business partners within your S/4HANA system, as well as in third-party systems. Whenever a new business partner is created, SAP Build triggers a process instance to validate the data, sending approval notifications to business users to assign addresses and payment information for the new business partner, ensuring high data quality.

By integrating event-driven architectures with SAP Build, you can significantly increase the processing speed of your business workflows, reduce human intervention, and enhance operational excellence for your IT department.

Here you can see a screenshot of the Advanced Event Mesh cockpit within SAP Integration Suite:

 

You can listen and subscribe to multiple events by creating queues in your SAP or non-SAP system within multiple regions and hyperscalers. For example it can be an event that indicates that a new business partner has been created within your ERP landscpape:

 

 

Once this subscription has been established you can proactively trigger a workflow or automation task within SAP Build Process Automation. To stay within the example, you can trigger new approval workflows once a new business partner has been established. As a result, every time a new business partner has been entered, an additional approver makes sure that the payment details and to address of the organization has been assigned correctly. By doing so you increase the degree of data accuracy and compliance of your business processes while minimizing the degree of human intervention:

 

Thanks to the native connectivity of SAP Build and our Event Mesh services you can easily select any event to trigger a process instance in SAP Build Process Automation. Further details on how to establish the connectivity can be found on the blogpost of my colleague Win, which can be found here or within the public documentation in the Help-Portal.

 

 

 

3rd scenario: Simplifying connectivity to improve developer efficiency

The final scenario for combining SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite involves our new consolidated API for SAP-managed data: Graph.

Graph uses modern open standards like OData v4 and GraphQL, serving as a key capability within API Management in SAP Integration Suite. With Graph, developers can access your business data as a single, semantically connected data graph that spans the entire suite of SAP products and beyond. It is designed for SAP’s ecosystem of partner and customer developers, offering a powerful API that significantly reduces the cost and complexity of creating and deploying reusable extensions and other client applications.

As enterprise landscapes continue to expand in both scale and complexity, each new system, SaaS, or microservice introduces new protocols, data models, connectivity requirements, and security conventions. Real-world challenges often span multiple lines of business, services, and APIs, making it difficult even for experienced developers to grasp all the underlying technologies and interfaces. Creating new business-extending client applications requires a growing range of expertise and skills. The rise of low-code tools, now widely adopted by nonprofessional developers, further widens this gap.

SAP Graph simplifies this complexity by allowing you to combine and expose data from multiple systems and data sources. You can then easily consume this API within SAP Build. At any point in your SAP Build Process Automation workflow, you can leverage an Action to retrieve, post, patch, put, or delete data within your SAP landscape.

This connectivity model allows you to interact with systems based on a unified enterprise data model, simplifying data access and avoiding unnecessary ETL processes and data replications for specific use cases.

Here you can also find a simplified architecture, that describes this scenario:

 

Additionally, SAP Integration Suite allows you to leverage proven integration features such as API Management, which helps monitor and evaluate your connections. You can also apply critical policies to these connections to ensure compliance and secure operations.

Further details can be found on the blogpost of my colleagues Palak Garg and Evgeniy Gorbunov.
